rationale: Re-verified commit c9f54530 in the bead worktree. The fenced-marker hostile probe now re-panels cleanly: exact markers are counted only as non-fenced whole-line comments, and the added test preserves the quoted body. Whitespace-mangled marker probes now reject as corrupt, and the shared helper asserts Create leaves both panel.json absent and BRIEF.md byte/metadata unchanged. The verdict-contract doc now states tally.go's actual behavior: any non-empty non-enum verdict is present but neither APPROVE nor REJECT, while empty/invalid verdicts are malformed. That matches tally.go's trim/uppercase parse and approve/reject-only switch. Targeted marker/doc tests and go test ./internal/panel pass.

findings.summary: Fence-quoted marker text in the BRIEF body no longer counts as duplicate managed markers; targeted re-panel test passes.
Whitespace-mangled markers are rejected as corrupt, with neither panel.json nor BRIEF.md touched.
Workflow interface docs now describe tally.go's real verdict parsing semantics without changing tally.go.
